blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Disorders of the Adrenal Gland?
Adrenal disorders เป็น endocrine emergency ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน neonatal period — หากพลาด อาจถึงชีวิตได้ภายในวันแรกของชีวิต. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) CAH 21-OHD salt-wasting crisis เกิดวันที่ 5–15 หลังคลอด — เด็กผู้ชายมักวินิจฉัยช้าเพราะ genitalia ปกติ; (2) Ambiguous genitalia = CAH จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่; (3) Preterm infant มี false positive 17-OHP ใน NBS → ต้องแปลผลตาม gestational age; (4) Relative adrenal insufficiency เป็น cause ของ vasopressor-resistant hypotension ที่รักษาได้; (5) Hydrocortisone + indomethacin → spontaneous GI perforation — combination อันตราย; (6) Adrenal crisis = emergency: ให้ IV hydrocortisone + saline + dextrose ก่อนรอผลแล็บ.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย dual embryologic origin ของ adrenal cortex (coelomic mesoderm) และ medulla (neural crest); รู้ fetal zone involution timeline
เข้าใจ 3 steroidogenic pathways: aldosterone (zona glomerulosa / renin-angiotensin), cortisol (zona fasciculata / HPA axis), androgens (zona reticularis)
รู้ role ของ fetal adrenal ใน fetoplacental unit: DHEAS → estriol; 3-HSD2 activity กำหนด cortisol vs DHEAS production
แปลผล neonatal adrenal function: age-specific 17-OHP values, false positive NBS ใน preterm, cortisol interpretation ใน critically ill
วินิจฉัยและรักษา 21-OHD CAH: classic vs salt-wasting, NBS, crisis management, hydrocortisone + fludrocortisone doses
แยก 5 forms ของ CAH: 21-OHD, 11-OHD, 17-OHD/17,20-lyase, 3-HSD2, Lipoid CAH — phenotype + key biochemical marker ของแต่ละชนิด
รู้ POR deficiency spectrum: mild cortisol deficiency → Antley-Bixler syndrome
วินิจฉัย Familial Glucocorticoid Deficiency (FGD): ↓ cortisol + ↑↑ ACTH + normal mineralocorticoid; mechanism + subtypes
รู้ AHC (X-linked NROB1/DAX-1 mutations), adrenal hemorrhage (right > left, Waterhouse-Friderichsen), IMAGe/MIRAGE syndromes
บริหาร adrenal crisis ครบ: initial resuscitation, sodium correction rate, IV hydrocortisone dose, maintenance therapy, stress dosing protocol
รู้ secondary/tertiary adrenal insufficiency: iatrogenic (steroids), developmental (septooptic dysplasia, pituitary hypoplasia), TPIT mutations
ใช้ stress dose protocol: fever/surgery/illness → 3× maintenance dose; MedicAlert bracelet ทุกราย
1. Embryology and Development of the Adrenal Gland
1.1 Dual Embryologic Origin
Adrenal cortex: มาจาก coelomic mesoderm ของ urogenital ridge; สัปดาห์ที่ 5: mesothelial cells proliferate → primitive adrenal cortex; สัปดาห์ที่ 6: second wave → adult (definitive) cortex
Adrenal medulla: มาจาก neural crest tissue ใน sympathetic ganglion (celiac plexus level) → migrate เข้า cortex สัปดาห์ที่ 8

Key transcription factors: SF-1 (NR5A1): essential for adrenal + gonad + hypothalamus development; SF-1 knockout mice = no adrenal + no gonad; DAX-1 (NROB1): colocalizes with SF-1; mutations → X-linked AHC
1.2 Fetal Zone ไปจนถึง Adult Zonation

rows: Encapsulated; outer definitive zone + large inner fetal zone
Fetal zone: DHEA + DHEAS → estriol (via placenta)
'Androgen factory' — rate of steroid secretion 5× adult at rest
ZG + ZF present; no ZR yet; adrenal = 8-9g (1/3 kidney weight)
Cortisol + aldosterone production; fetal zone begins involution

blocks.text: 1.3 Adrenal Morphology ที่ Birth
Weight: 8–9 g; approximately 1/3 size of neonatal kidney; 10–20× larger relative to body weight than adult (0.4% vs 0.01% body weight)
Ultrasound appearance: thin reflective core (medulla) + thick transonic zone (cortex); characteristic neonatal finding
Fetal zone involutes → gland reaches ~8% of kidney size in adulthood
2. Adrenal Steroidogenesis — 3 Pathways
2.1 Steroidogenic Cascade (Adult Adrenal)

Cholesterol (substrate) → StAR (steroidogenic acute regulatory protein) transfers cholesterol to inner mitochondrial membrane → CYP11A1 (SCC) cleaves side chain → Pregnenolone. 3 pathways diverge from here:

rows: HPA axis: CRH → ACTH → cortisol (negative feedback)

blocks.text: 2.2 HPA Axis — Regulation ของ Cortisol
Stress / circadian → CRH + AVP (hypothalamic paraventricular nucleus) → pituitary → ACTH → adrenal → cortisol → negative feedback ที่ hypothalamus + pituitary
Circadian variation: ไม่มีจนถึง 6 months of age → random cortisol sampling ใน neonates interpret ยาก
ACTH receptor (MC2R, melanocortin 2 receptor): present ใน fetal zone + transitional zone + adult adrenal cortex

ถ้า cortisol ↓ → ACTH ↑ (negative feedback หาย)
กลไกสำคัญ: ถ้า adrenal dysfunction (เช่น CAH) → cortisol ไม่ถูกสร้าง → ACTH ↑↑ → กระตุ้น steroidogenic pathway ที่ยัง intact → substrate สะสม upstream + shunting ไป androgen pathway. นี่คือ mechanism ของ virilization ใน 21-OHD.
2.3 Fetoplacental Unit — บทบาทของ Fetal Adrenal
Fetal adrenal = "androgen factory" ใน 2nd + 3rd trimester: ผลิต DHEA + DHEAS มหาศาล (rate 5× adult at rest)
Fetoplacental unit pathway: Fetal adrenal DHEAS → (placental sulfatase) → DHEA → (placental 3-HSD1, aromatase) → Estradiol / Estriol; Fetal liver 16-hydroxylates DHEAS → 16-DHEA → placenta → Estriol
Near-term pregnancy: estrogen production ↑ 1000-fold กว่า non-pregnant women; fetoplacental unit provides most estrogen after 8 weeks

3-HSD2 activity timeline (critical): (1) 7–12 wk: 3-HSD2 transiently high → ↑ cortisol → suppress DHEAS → prevent female virilization; (2) End of 1st trimester – 3rd trimester: 3-HSD2 ↓ → ↑ DHEAS secretion; (3) Near term: 3-HSD2 ↑ again → ↑ cortisol → lung maturation (surfactant)
Maternal cortisol protection: placental 11β-HSD2 oxidizes maternal cortisol → inactive cortisone → fetus ไม่ถูกกดจาก maternal cortisol
3. Assessing Adrenal Function in the Newborn
3.1 Age-Specific Steroid Values
Adrenal steroid levels vary significantly with gestational age + postnatal age; ต้องใช้ normative data ที่ match age เสมอ
Preterm infants: sustained elevation ของ 17-OHP + ↑ 17-OHP:cortisol ratio → reflects ↓ 21-hydroxylase activity (immature); NBS 17-OHP often abnormal → false positive ใน preterm = common
Aldosterone: preterm > term > older children > adults; reflects mineralocorticoid resistance in neonates (especially preterm)
Cortisol: no diurnal variation until 6 months; cortisol sulfate (excreted as sulfate esters ≥ 1 month postnatally) may interfere with immunoassays
3.2 Relative Adrenal Insufficiency in Critically Ill Neonate
Definition: HPA axis produces inadequate cortisol relative to degree of illness/stress; preterm infants particularly at risk (immature HPA + adrenal)

Lab: critically ill neonates มักมี low cortisol + low ACTH ทั้งคู่ — ไม่ใช่ true primary adrenal failure; ACTH stimulation test → usually normal adrenal response (problem is upstream HPA, not adrenal)
ELBW (500–999g): low cortisol ≠ predict mortality/morbidity; HIGH basal cortisol → associated with severe IVH; extremely elevated → morbidity + death (stress marker)
Therapeutic trial: vasopressor-resistant hypotension + cardiac hypofunction → consider hydrocortisone 1 mg/kg trial; discontinue if no response or pretreatment cortisol > 15 μg/dL

⚠️ DANGER: Hydrocortisone + Indomethacin = GI Perforation Risk
Concurrent use of hydrocortisone + indomethacin → spontaneous gastrointestinal perforation — avoid this combination. ถ้าจำเป็นต้องใช้ทั้งสอง → discuss risk-benefit carefully. Meta-analysis (Higgins Perinatol 2010): hydrocortisone ↑ BP + ↓ vasopressor need, แต่ long-term effects unknown.
4. Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) — Framework

CAH = family of autosomal recessive inherited disorders with defect in one enzymatic step ของ cortisol synthesis from cholesterol. ผลที่ตามมา: ↓ cortisol → ↑ ACTH → hyperplasia ของ adrenal → substrate สะสม + shunting ไป intact pathways.

rows: Ambiguous genitalia BOTH sexes; salt-wasting possible
No steroid synthesis; 46,XY = phenotypic female (no uterus — AMH intact)

blocks.text: 5. 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency (21-OHD) — Most Common CAH
5.1 Epidemiology + Genetics
Most common CAH: 90–95% ของทุก CAH cases; classic form incidence: 1:15,000 live births (worldwide NBS data; some populations 1:7,500)

Gene: CYP21A2 — microsomal cytochrome P450 on chromosome 6p (within HLA complex); >100 mutations; flanked by pseudogene CYP21A1P; unequal crossing over + gene conversion = most common mutation mechanisms
Genotype-phenotype: 79–88% predictive accuracy; some discordance within mutation-identical groups (modifier genes)
5.2 Pathophysiology
CYP21A2 deficiency → ↓ conversion ของ 17-OHP → 11-deoxycortisol (cortisol pathway) AND ↓ conversion progesterone → deoxycorticosterone (aldosterone pathway)
Result: cortisol ↓ → ACTH ↑↑ → 17-OHP ↑↑↑ → shunted to androgen pathway → ↑↑ androgens → virilization ใน female fetus (before week 12!)
Two classic forms: (1) Simple virilizing (25%): cortisol ↓ but enough aldosterone; (2) Salt-wasting (75%): both cortisol + aldosterone ↓ → adrenal crisis
5.3 Clinical Presentation
Females: ambiguous genitalia at birth (clitoromegaly ± labioscrotal fusion); ← virilization occurs before 12 weeks gestation while HPA active
Males (classic): normal genitalia at birth → often diagnosed late (NBS or salt-wasting crisis); salt-wasting 46,XY males: most likely to present in crisis if NBS not available
Salt-wasting crisis: days 5–15 of life; hyponatremia + hyperkalemia + ↑ plasma renin activity + volume depletion → potentially fatal
Long-term untreated: tall child (↑ androgens ↑ growth early) → short adult (↑ estrogens close epiphyses); males: testicular adrenal rest tumors + infertility
5.4 Diagnosis
NBS: blood-spot 17-OHP; preterm → false positive common (↑ 17-OHP from immature 21-hydroxylase) → follow-up testing required
Confirmatory: serum 17-OHP ↑↑↑ (baseline); ACTH stimulation (cosyntropin) 15 mcg/kg IV (< 2 yr) up to 125 mcg; or 250 mcg (> 2 yr); sample at baseline + 60 min
Molecular: CYP21A2 gene mutation analysis (confirm genotype + guide prognosis)

ACTH Stimulation Test ใน Neonates: Dose Summary
Cosyntropin (synthetic ACTH): < 2 years: 15 mcg/kg IV/IM, maximum 125 mcg; > 2 years: 250 mcg. Draw at baseline and 60 minutes. ค่า 17-OHP ที่ stimulated > 10,000 ng/dL = classic 21-OHD มาก. ค่า preterm baseline สูง → ดู Table 84.1 normative ranges by GA.
5.5 Management ของ 21-OHD CAH

Glucocorticoid replacement: Hydrocortisone 15–20 mg/m²/day ÷ 3 doses (initial; may be higher in newborn); ↓ to 10–15 mg/m²/day after initial ACTH suppression; หลีกเลี่ยง synthetic steroids (growth suppression)

Mineralocorticoid (salt-wasting form): Fludrocortisone (9α-fludrocortisone, Florinef) 0.05–0.4 mg/day oral; neonates have mineralocorticoid resistance → higher dose needed; NB: no parenteral fludrocortisone available → give high-dose IV hydrocortisone instead (20 mg HC = 0.1 mg fludrocortisone equivalent)
Sodium supplement: 1–2 g NaCl/day added to feeds (breast milk + formula ≈ 8 mEq Na/L = insufficient); 1g NaCl = 17 mEq Na

Prenatal treatment (experimental): dexamethasone to pregnant mother before 6 weeks post conception → ↓ virilization in affected 46,XX fetus; CONTROVERSIAL — all endocrine societies consider experimental; negative effects on verbal working memory and executive function reported (Swedish studies); 7 fetuses treated unnecessarily for every 1 benefit
Goal of monitoring: suppress ACTH but avoid iatrogenic Cushing syndrome; 17-OHP normalization should NOT be the target (would require over-replacement)
6. Other Forms of CAH
6.1 11β-Hydroxylase Deficiency (11-OHD)
Frequency: 5–8% of all CAH; 1:100,000 general; 1:5,000–7,000 in Moroccan Jews (founder effect); 13.5% of CAH in Turkey
Gene: CYP11B1 (chromosome 8q); >100 mutations; predominantly point mutations (unlike 21-OHD)
Pathophysiology: ↓ conversion 11-deoxycortisol → cortisol + progesterone → deoxycorticosterone (DOC); DOC is mineralocorticoid active → sodium retention → HYPERTENSION
Key lab: ↑↑ DOC + ↑↑ 11-deoxycortisol (ACTH stimulation); renin normal or ↓ (DOC suppresses renin); hypokalemia not universal
NB: 17-OHP may also be ↑ (high 11-deoxycortisol cross-reacts) → NBS false positive for 21-OHD
Treatment: glucocortocoid same as 21-OHD; NO fludrocortisone needed (hypertension resolves with cortisol replacement → ↓ DOC; mineralocorticoid production often excess)
6.2 17-Hydroxylase / 17,20-Lyase Deficiency
Gene: CYP17A1; ~1% of CAH; 1:50,000; higher in Brazil + Netherlands + Japan (founder effects)
Defect affects BOTH 17-hydroxylation AND 17,20-lyase reactions simultaneously (usually)
Can't make cortisol, BUT makes large amounts corticosterone (weak GC) + DOC → mitigates AI partially + causes HYPERTENSION + hypokalemia
Can't make androgens/estrogens: 46,XY → undervirilized at birth (DSD); 46,XX → primary amenorrhea + hypogonadism
Treatment: GC suppresses excessive mineralocorticoid → normalizes BP; sex steroid replacement for adults; abdominal testes (46,XY) → remove (malignancy risk)
6.3 3β-Hydroxysteroid Dehydrogenase Type 2 Deficiency (3-HSD2)
Gene: HSD3B2 (chromosome 1p13); autosomal recessive; ~40 mutations known
Enzyme converts 3β-hydroxy-5 steroids → 3-keto-4 steroids → essential for ALL steroid pathways
Unique: BOTH gonads AND adrenals affected → ambiguous genitalia in BOTH sexes
46,XY males: micropenis + perineal hypospadias + bifid scrotum + ± blind vaginal pouch; 46,XX females: clitoromegaly (peripheral conversion of 5α-steroids → androgens)
Salt-wasting form: severe mutations; mild mutations → premature pubic hair ± hirsutism
6.4 Lipoid Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (StAR Deficiency)

Most severe CAH: StAR protein (steroidogenic acute regulatory protein) can't transfer cholesterol into inner mitochondrial membrane → NO steroid synthesis at all; lipid droplets accumulate in adrenocortical cells
Gene: STAR (8p11.2); most common in Japanese + Palestinian populations (founder effect); also CYP11A1 mutations (similar phenotype, no hyperplasia)
46,XY phenotype: phenotypically female (Leydig cell destruction → ↓ testosterone); BUT Sertoli cell intact → AMH secreted → Müllerian regression → no uterus/fallopian tubes
46,XX ovary: initially spared (steroidogenesis delayed until puberty) → eventually damaged by tropic hormones
6.5 POR (Cytochrome P450 Oxidoreductase) Deficiency
Autosomal recessive; affects P450c17, P450c21, P450-aro (all require POR as electron donor)
Spectrum: mild cortisol deficiency → classic Antley-Bixler syndrome (ABS)

ABS features: craniosynostosis, hydrocephalus, choanal stenosis/atresia, radiohumeral synostosis, bowing of long bones, arachnodactyly, clubfeet, renal anomalies; skeletal changes possibly from ↓ CYP26B1 (retinoic acid degradation)
Genital: ambiguous in males AND females; maternal virilization during pregnancy (from ↑ placental androgens) + ↓ maternal estriol
ABS without steroidogenesis abnormality = FGFR2 mutation (not POR); genetic testing distinguishes
7. Other Primary Adrenal Disorders
7.1 Familial Glucocorticoid Deficiency (FGD) — ACTH Resistance
Autosomal recessive; ACTH receptor resistance → ↓ cortisol, ↑↑↑ ACTH; mineralocorticoid NORMAL (aldosterone under renin-angiotensin → preserved)
Genetics: FGD1 = MC2R (melanocortin 2 receptor) mutations (~50%); FGD2 = MRAP mutations; ~10% = NNT mutations; also MCM4, TXNRD2, GPX1; ~1/3 unknown
Presentation: neonatal hypoglycemia (responds to frequent feeds); hyperpigmentation (↑↑ ACTH → ↑ melanocyte stimulation); TALL stature identified later
Lab: serum cortisol ↓ or undetectable + ACTH ↑↑↑ + electrolytes NORMAL
7.2 Triple A Syndrome (Allgrove Syndrome)
Triad (in order of appearance): (1) Alacrima → (2) Achalasia → (3) Adrenal insufficiency
Gene: AAAS → ALADIN protein; WD-repeat family; ALADIN participates in nuclear translocation of ferritin heavy chain → prevents oxidative nuclear damage
Progressive neurological dysfunction: polyneuropathy, deafness, mental retardation, hyperkeratosis palms/soles; presents first decade of life
7.3 Neonatal Adrenoleukodystrophy (NALD) + X-ALD
NALD: peroxisome biogenesis disorder (PEX1 mutations = most common); VLCFA accumulation + adrenal atrophy + white matter degeneration
Facial features: dolichocephaly, high forehead, esotropia, epicanthic folds, low-set ears, broad nasal bridge; death in early childhood

X-ALD: X-linked (ABCD1 gene, Xq28); also VLCFA accumulation + adrenal insufficiency + CNS deterioration; on NBS in some US states; early dx → HSCT or gene therapy; can present from childhood to adulthood
7.4 Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome
Gene: DHCR7 (chromosome 11q12-q13); autosomal recessive; ↓ 7-dehydrocholesterol C-7 reductase → can't synthesize cholesterol → ↓ all steroids + ↓ cell membrane synthesis
Incidence: 1:20,000–40,000; less common in Asian/African ancestry
Biochemical: ↓ cholesterol + ↑↑ 7-dehydrocholesterol (diagnostic)
Features: dysmorphic facies, microcephaly, syndactyly 2nd-3rd toes, cardiac defects, 46,XY undervirilized genitalia, photosensitivity, mental retardation
In utero: ↓ maternal estriol + ↑ large fetal adrenal glands (on US)
Treatment: cholesterol supplementation tried but doesn't cross BBB → no effect on developmental delay; simvastatin (RCT): ↑ cholesterol:7-dehydrocholesterol ratio + ↑ irritability, relatively safe
7.5 Adrenal Hypoplasia Congenita (AHC)
Incidence: ~1:12,500 births; 4 forms:
(1) Sporadic + pituitary hypoplasia: unknown gene
(2) Autosomal recessive (miniature adult cortex): small glands, normal adult architecture; genetic basis unknown
(3) X-linked cytomegalic form (NROB1/DAX-1 mutations): absence of permanent zone + large disorganized fetal cells; +hypogonadotropic hypogonadism; presents neonatal–infancy
(4) X-linked + glycerol kinase deficiency + DMD contiguous deletion (Xp21): psychomotor retardation + muscular dystrophy + hypertelorism
X-linked AHC (NROB1): glucocorticoid + mineralocorticoid deficiency; delayed puberty from hypogonadotropic hypogonadism; phenotypic variation does NOT correlate with genotype
7.6 Adrenal Hemorrhage
Incidence: 1.7/1000 autopsied infants; up to 3% on abdominal US
Right adrenal most common (compressed between liver + spine; left more mobile)
Causes: birth trauma, sepsis, coagulopathies, ischemia, shock
Presentation: (1) asymptomatic → incidental adrenal calcification; (2) abdominal mass + anemia + jaundice; (3) adrenal insufficiency: hypoglycemia + hypotension + hypothermia + apnea + shock

Waterhouse-Friderichsen Syndrome
Meningococcal septicemia + bilateral adrenal hemorrhage → circulatory collapse. Also: herpes simplex, Pseudomonas, Bacteroides, echovirus 11/6. Septic shock (especially SGA infants) → adrenal hemorrhage ± rhabdomyolysis + renal insufficiency. treat adrenal insufficiency aggressively ใน septic shock neonates.
7.7 Rare Syndromic Disorders with Adrenal Insufficiency
Wolman disease: lysosomal acid esterase deficiency; AR; 1:350,000; hepatosplenomegaly + vomiting + diarrhea + growth failure; bilateral adrenal calcifications = pathognomonic; fatal year 1
IMAGe syndrome: CDKN1C gain-of-function mutations (chr 11p); Intrauterine growth retardation + Metaphyseal dysplasia + Adrenal hypoplasia congenita + Genital anomalies; hypercalciuria ± hypercalcemia
MIRAGE syndrome: SAMD9 mutations; Myelodysplasia + Infections + Restricted growth + Adrenal hypoplasia + Genital anomalies + Enteropathy; poor prognosis
Mitochondrial disorders (Kearns-Sayre): large-scale mtDNA deletions; adrenal insufficiency + short stature + hypogonadism + diabetes + hypothyroidism + lactic acidosis + myopathy
8. Secondary/Tertiary Adrenal Insufficiency + Adrenal Crisis
8.1 Iatrogenic Adrenal Insufficiency
Supraphysiologic glucocorticoids (BPD treatment) → HPA axis suppression → corticotroph atrophy → secondary adrenal insufficiency
Even single course prenatal betamethasone → suppresses stress reactivity in healthy newborns
Recovery of HPA axis after GC discontinuation: highly variable; evidence of suppression > 1 year in some cases; pituitary recovers before hypothalamus and adrenals (preterm same as adults)
8.2 Developmental Adrenal Insufficiency

Septooptic dysplasia (de Morsier syndrome): HESX1, SOX2 mutations; optic nerve hypoplasia + absent septum pellucidum + pituitary hypoplasia; concomitant diabetes insipidus → ↑ risk of sudden death in childhood
Pituitary hypoplasia/aplasia, hydrencephaly, anencephaly → absent ACTH → adrenal atrophy
Multiple pituitary hormone deficiency: HESX1, LHX4, SOX3, PROP1 mutations; ACTH deficiency part of multiplex
Isolated ACTH deficiency: TPIT mutations (T-box transcription factor → controls POMC in corticotrophs only → adrenal-only phenotype); ~50% of patients have no identified mutation
Neonatal signs of hypopituitarism: hypoglycemia + prolonged jaundice + shock + microphallus (males)
Central AI: NO mineralocorticoid replacement needed (aldosterone under renin-angiotensin, not ACTH); evaluate for other pituitary hormone deficiencies
8.3 Adrenal Crisis — Initial Management

Adrenal Crisis Protocol — เริ่มรักษาก่อนรอผล!
ขั้นตอน: (1) Draw blood: electrolytes, aldosterone, renin, cortisol, ACTH → (2) IV fluid: NS + 5–10% dextrose (restore cardiovascular stability + correct hypoglycemia). (3) Hydrocortisone IV: 50–100 mg/m² bolus → 50–100 mg/m²/day ÷ q6–8h จนกว่า stable. (4) Sodium correction ≤ 0.5 mEq/L/hour (risk: central pontine myelinolysis). Na deficit = (140 – patient Na) × 0.6 × weight(kg).
8.4 Maintenance Therapy

Normal daily cortisol production: 6–7 mg/m²/day (children + adolescents); oral hydrocortisone absorption ~60% → target 10–12 mg/m²/day oral ÷ 3 doses daily (3 doses, NOT 2 — avoids hypoglycemia from low morning cortisol)
Fludrocortisone: 0.05–0.4 mg/day oral (dose does NOT increase with growth — aldosterone production stable after 1 month); NaCl supplement 1–2 g/day (formula/breast milk ≈ 8 mEq Na/L = low)

Hydrocortisone preferred over synthetic steroids (fewer growth-suppressive effects); smallest tablet = 5 mg (can cut to quarters = 1.25 mg); new formulation: 0.5/1/2 mg tablets (oral only — not via NG/GT)
Note: FDA withdrew oral hydrocortisone suspension (poor absorption) — avoid compounded suspensions
8.5 Stress Dosing Protocol

rows: 30–100 mg/m² IV on call to OR (before anesthesia)
Or infuse continuously in IV fluids during surgery
Continue 24–48h post-op; NO mineralocorticoid needed if adequate salt balance

blocks.text: Every patient: MedicAlert bracelet/necklace + emergency card: diagnosis + daily medications + doses + physician contact. Patients and families MUST have stress dosing instructions
9. NICU Practical — การจัดการ Adrenal ใน NICU

Ambiguous genitalia at birth: CAH until proven otherwise. Send: serum 17-OHP, cortisol, ACTH, electrolytes, aldosterone, renin + karyotype + pelvic/abdominal US; consult endocrinology + genetics + urology. DO NOT assign sex until evaluation complete.
Nonpalpable testes + suspected 46,XY: evaluate for CAH (17-OHD, lipoid CAH) + rule out cryptorchidism vs DSD; US/MRI for internal anatomy

Salt-wasting crisis (day 5–15): hyponatremia + hyperkalemia + shock in a MALE infant = 21-OHD CAH until proven otherwise. Start NS + dextrose + IV hydrocortisone 50–100 mg/m²; send 17-OHP, renin, aldosterone, cortisol BEFORE giving steroids if possible
Preterm infant with ↑ 17-OHP on NBS: do NOT diagnose CAH based on NBS alone; confirm with serum 17-OHP + gestational age-matched normative data + ACTH stimulation if needed
Vasopressor-resistant hypotension: consider relative adrenal insufficiency → hydrocortisone 1 mg/kg trial; avoid combination with indomethacin → GI perforation
Adrenal hemorrhage on US: asymmetric adrenal + difficult delivery + anemia/jaundice → check cortisol; right side most common (liver compressed); Waterhouse-Friderichsen in sepsis
Prior steroid exposure (prenatal betamethasone, postnatal dex/HC): assume HPA suppression; stress-dose for surgery/critical illness; monitor for HPA recovery > 1 year in prolonged therapy cases
FGD suspect: ↓ cortisol + ↑↑↑ ACTH + normal electrolytes + hypoglycemia in neonate → MC2R/MRAP gene testing; treat with hydrocortisone only (no fludrocortisone)
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Male Infant Day 5–15 + Shock + Hyponatremia = CAH Until Proven Otherwise
ผู้ชายที่เกิดปกติ แต่วันที่ 5–15 มี feeding poor + poor weight gain + เอียง + hyponatremia + hyperkalemia + high renin + shock = 21-OHD salt-wasting CAH จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Male ไม่มี ambiguous genitalia → diagnosed late → crisis สูงกว่า female. Send 17-OHP + start IV HC + NS + dextrose ทันที. อย่ารอผล.

PEARL 2 — Preterm 17-OHP High on NBS ≠ CAH Until Confirmed
Preterm infants มี immature 21-hydroxylase + elevated 17-OHP physiologically → NBS false positive common. ไม่วินิจฉัย CAH จาก NBS alone ใน preterm; confirm ด้วย serum 17-OHP เทียบกับ gestational-age matched normative values + cosyntropin stimulation. Aldosterone ก็สูงใน preterm normally — interpret with context.

PEARL 3 — 11-OHD vs 21-OHD: ความต่างคือ Hypertension
ทั้ง 21-OHD และ 11-OHD virilize ♀ — แยกด้วย blood pressure + renin. 21-OHD salt-wasting = ↓ BP + ↑↑ renin + hyponatremia; 11-OHD = ↑ BP + ↓/normal renin + hypokalemia (variable). Key biochemistry: 11-OHD has ↑↑ 11-deoxycortisol + ↑↑ DOC. 11-OHD treatment: GC only — ไม่ต้อง fludrocortisone (DOC provides mineralocorticoid activity).

PEARL 4 — Sodium Correction Rate: ≤ 0.5 mEq/L/hour
Adrenal crisis มักมี severe hyponatremia; correct too fast → central pontine myelinolysis. Target: ≤ 0.5–1 mEq/L/hour; Na deficit = (140 – patient Na) × 0.6 × weight. NS + dextrose เป็น fluid of choice; monitor serum Na q2–4h ช่วงแรก. DO NOT give hypertonic saline unless severe symptomatic hyponatremia (seizure).

PEARL 5 — Stress Dose = 3× Maintenance; Sick Day Rule
ทุก patient ที่มี adrenal insufficiency (primary OR secondary) ต้องรู้ sick day rule: fever / vomiting / surgery / significant illness → triple the maintenance dose = 30–100 mg/m²/day; ถ้าอาเจียน → IM hydrocortisone ทันที; continue until 24h symptom-free. MedicAlert bracelet + emergency card ทุกราย; family must be trained before discharge.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Speiser PW et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2018;103:4043 (Endocrine Society CPG): Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-OHD — comprehensive guideline: dosing, monitoring, NBS, prenatal treatment. Hydrocortisone preferred over synthetic GC; prenatal dex = experimental.

Pang SY et al. Pediatrics 1988;81:866 (NBS data): Worldwide experience in NBS for classic 21-OHD — established incidence 1:15,000; demonstrated NBS effectiveness; baseline reference for false positive rates.

New MI et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2001;86:5651: Prenatal diagnosis + treatment of CAH in 532 pregnancies — dexamethasone before 6 weeks reduces virilization; ethics controversy regarding experimental status.

Lajic S et al. Horm Res Paediatr 2018;89:362: Prenatal dex for CAH long-term follow-up — negative effect on executive function and social behavior in treated children; reinforces experimental classification.

Hirvikoski T et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2007;92:542: Prenatal dex and cognitive outcomes — decreased verbal working memory + decreased self-perception of scholastic ability in non-CAH treated children; dose of caution for prenatal treatment.

Higgins S, Friedlich P, Seri I. J Perinatol 2010;30:373 (meta-analysis): Hydrocortisone for hypotension in preterm — improved BP + less vasopressor need; clinical benefit uncertain; long-term effects unknown; hydrocortisone + indomethacin → GI perforation risk.

Fernandez EF, Watterberg KL. J Perinatol 2009;29:S44: Relative adrenal insufficiency in preterm and term newborns — ACTH stimulation usually normal (adrenal intact; upstream HPA dysfunction); hydrocortisone 1 mg/kg trial for vasopressor-resistant hypotension.

Aucott SW et al. Pediatrics 2008;122:775: Cortisol concentrations in ELBW infants (500–999g) — low cortisol NOT predictive of adverse outcomes; HIGH basal cortisol = severe IVH; extremely elevated = morbidity/death (stress marker).

Sippell WG et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1978;46:971: Longitudinal study of neonatal adrenal steroids at birth — established normative data for aldosterone, cortisol, 17-OHP at various gestational ages; reference for interpreting neonatal adrenal function.

Mesiano S, Jaffe RB. Endocr Rev 1997;18:378: Developmental biology of primate fetal adrenal — established fetal zone involution timeline, 3-HSD2 regulation, DHEAS fetoplacental role; foundational reference.
Miller WL, Auchus RJ. Endocr Rev 2011;32:81: Molecular biology of human steroidogenesis — comprehensive review of all CYP enzymes, StAR, adrenal pathways; explains mechanism of each CAH form.

El-Khairi R et al. Endocr Dev 2011;20:38: DAX-1 (NROB1) and SF-1 (NR5A1) in human adrenal function — DAX-1 mutations in 58% of XY AHC cases; NR5A1 mutations rare cause of adrenal failure; review of transcription factor roles.

Bornstein SR. N Engl J Med 2009;360:2328: Predisposing factors for adrenal insufficiency — comprehensive review: infection (TB, CMV, HIV), autoimmune, surgical, drug-induced, genetic; includes stress dosing rationale.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Male term infant วันที่ 10 หลังคลอด ซึม ไม่ดูดนม น้ำหนักลง Na 125, K 7.2, glucose 40 mg/dL — diagnose อะไร? workup และ immediate management?

ตอบ: Classic presentation: 21-OHD salt-wasting CAH ใน male infant (genitalia ปกติ → มักวินิจฉัยช้า). Draw stat: 17-OHP, cortisol, ACTH, aldosterone, renin, electrolytes, glucose, karyotype. Management IMMEDIATELY: NS + 10% dextrose IV → restore volume + glucose; hydrocortisone IV 50–100 mg/m²; correct Na ≤ 0.5 mEq/L/hr (CPM risk). Once stable: fludrocortisone 0.05–0.4 mg/day + NaCl supplement. Confirm: CYP21A2 molecular testing.
Q: แยก 21-OHD salt-wasting กับ 11-OHD ด้วยอะไร? treatment ต่างกันไหม?

ตอบ: แยกด้วย: (1) Blood pressure: 21-OHD = ↓ BP; 11-OHD = ↑ BP (hypertension); (2) Renin: 21-OHD = ↑↑; 11-OHD = ↓/normal (DOC suppresses renin); (3) Key biochemistry: 21-OHD = ↑↑ 17-OHP; 11-OHD = ↑↑ 11-deoxycortisol + ↑↑ DOC. Treatment: 21-OHD = HC + fludrocortisone + NaCl; 11-OHD = HC only — ไม่ต้อง fludrocortisone (DOC มี mineralocorticoid activity → มักเป็น HTN ไม่ใช่ salt-wasting).
Q: Preterm 28 weeks 17-OHP บน NBS สูง — ควรทำอะไรต่อ?

ตอบ: Preterm infant มี physiologically elevated 17-OHP เนื่องจาก immature 21-hydroxylase + fetal zone involution incomplete → NBS false positive common. ไม่วินิจฉัย CAH จาก NBS alone. ขั้นตอน: (1) Confirm serum 17-OHP เทียบ gestational-age matched normative data; (2) ดู clinical signs (electrolytes, BP, genital exam); (3) ACTH stimulation (cosyntropin 15 mcg/kg) หาก serum 17-OHP borderline; (4) CYP21A2 gene testing ถ้า stimulated 17-OHP elevated significantly.
Q: Lipoid CAH ใน 46,XY infant — ทำไมถึงเป็น phenotypically female ทั้งที่ไม่มี uterus?

ตอบ: Lipoid CAH (StAR mutations): ↓ cholesterol transport → no steroid synthesis → Leydig cells ทำ testosterone ไม่ได้ → 46,XY ไม่ virilize → phenotypically female. แต่ Sertoli cell ยัง intact → AMH (anti-Müllerian hormone) ยังถูกหลั่ง → Müllerian structures regress → ไม่มี uterus + fallopian tubes. Result: phenotypically female but no uterus. Ovary in 46,XX initially spared (steroidogenesis delayed until puberty → then damaged by LH/FSH stimulation).
Q: FGD ต่างจาก 21-OHD อย่างไร? biochemistry และ treatment?

ตอบ: FGD = ACTH receptor resistance → ↓ cortisol + ↑↑↑ ACTH; mineralocorticoid NORMAL (aldosterone under renin-angiotensin → not affected); electrolytes normal; no salt-wasting crisis. 21-OHD = enzyme deficiency → ↓ cortisol + ↓ aldosterone (salt-wasting) OR ↓ cortisol only (simple virilizing). FGD treatment: hydrocortisone replacement only — no fludrocortisone needed; no genital abnormality. Genes: FGD1 = MC2R; FGD2 = MRAP; ~10% = NNT mutations.
Q: Adrenal crisis management: sodium ควรแก้เร็วแค่ไหน? คำนวณ Na deficit อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Rate: ≤ 0.5–1 mEq/L/hour (เกินนี้ → risk central pontine myelinolysis). Formula Na deficit: (140 – patient Na) × 0.6 × weight (kg). ตัวอย่าง: Na 118, weight 3 kg → (140–118) × 0.6 × 3 = 22 × 1.8 = 39.6 mEq Na deficit; แก้ใน ≥ 40 hours ที่ 0.5 mEq/L/hr (22 mEq ÷ 0.5 mEq/L/hr). Fluid: NS + 5–10% dextrose; hydrocortisone IV 50–100 mg/m² bolus → 50–100 mg/m²/day ÷ q6–8h.
Q: 17-Hydroxylase deficiency ใน 46,XY newborn — present อย่างไร? biochemistry อะไรที่จำเพาะ?

ตอบ: 46,XY: born as phenotypic female (undervirilized) — no testosterone synthesis (17,20-lyase blocked). Biochemistry: ↑↑ corticosterone + ↑↑ deoxycorticosterone (DOC) → mineralocorticoid excess → HYPERTENSION + hypokalemia; renin ↓; androgens ↓; estrogens ↓. Cortisol ↓ but corticosterone (weak GC) mitigates AI. Contrast: 21-OHD has ↑ 17-OHP; 17-OHD has ↑ corticosterone (not 17-OHP). Treatment: GC suppresses DOC → normalizes BP.
Q: X-ALD vs NALD: แยกอย่างไร? treatment?

ตอบ: X-ALD: X-linked (ABCD1 gene, Xq28); males primarily; VLCFA accumulation; adrenal insufficiency + CNS demyelination; can present childhood–adulthood; on NBS in some US states; early dx → HSCT or gene therapy. NALD: autosomal recessive peroxisome biogenesis disorder (PEX1 most common); present at birth (dysmorphic features: dolichocephaly, high forehead, low-set ears); fatal early childhood; no effective treatment. Both: VLCFA elevated on testing. Key diff: genetics + severity + survival.
Q: Secondary vs primary adrenal insufficiency: mineralocorticoid replacement ต่างกันอย่างไร? ทำไม?

ตอบ: Primary AI: adrenal gland itself damaged → ↓ BOTH cortisol + aldosterone → requires glucocorticoid + mineralocorticoid replacement + NaCl. Secondary/tertiary AI: pituitary/hypothalamic problem → ↓ ACTH but renin-angiotensin system intact → aldosterone production preserved → NO fludrocortisone needed. Evaluate secondary AI patients for other pituitary hormone deficiencies (GH, TSH, LH/FSH, ADH).
Q: Stress dosing rules: เมื่อไหร่ต้อง triple dose? dose เท่าไหร่? นานแค่ไหน?

ตอบ: Indications: fever + GI illness + significant illness + surgery. Dose: triple maintenance = 30–100 mg/m²/day ÷ q6–8h. Duration: until 24h symptom-free (fever/illness) OR 24–48h post-operative. ถ้าอาเจียน: IM/IV hydrocortisone immediately (cannot rely on oral). Surgery pre-op: 30–100 mg/m² IV on call to OR. MedicAlert + emergency card mandatory; family must know and practice sick day rules before discharge.
Day 85 Preview

Avery Chapter 85 — Differences in Sex Development (DSD)
พรุ่งนี้: Differences in Sex Development (DSD) — หลังเรียน adrenal disorders (รวมถึง CAH ที่ cause DSD) วันนี้ เราจะเข้าสู่ systematic approach to DSD: classification (46,XX DSD / 46,XY DSD / sex chromosome DSD), karyotype-guided workup, role of ultrasound + hormone profiles, timing of sex assignment, multidisciplinary DSD team, ethical considerations ใน ambiguous genitalia — เชื่อมต่อกับ 17-OHD, lipoid CAH, 3-HSD2 deficiency ที่เพิ่งเรียน.
